strengths: 19+ years of hands-on software engineering across web, mobile, embedded, and low-latency backend systems (exceeds the 15-year minimum)
Entrepreneurial track record: founder of GISCollective, an open-source geo-data platform used in production by Green Map since 2016
Current work on AI-agent infrastructure at re:cinq, including MCP code generators and frameworks for agentic workflows
concerns: No evidence of recognized AI/ML expertise or a demonstrated AI-powered product or platform at scale, a core preferred qualification
Career history is senior/staff IC delivery roles; no evidence of director-level scope, strategic advisory to customers, or org-wide technical influence
Cloud experience is applied (AWS CDK, Kubernetes deployments) rather than cloud platform strategy or future-state product definition

key_factors.factor: Recognized AI expertise and AI products built

response.justification: The role's core competencies are AI strategy leadership, cloud future-state vision, recognized AI expertise, and executive-level influence. The candidate clearly meets the minimums: a BS in Computer Science and roughly 19 years of engineering experience spanning high-throughput systems (Dunnhumby, 'millions of HTTP requests a second') and full-stack product work. He also satisfies the entrepreneurial preference as founder of GISCollective and shows emerging AI relevance through MCP tooling and agentic-workflow frameworks at re:cinq. However, the résumé contains no evidence of shipping a recognized AI product or platform, no strategic advisory or engineering-leadership scope beyond senior/staff IC work, and his public speaking (FOSS4G, testing workshops) is practitioner-level rather than industry-shaping AI thought leadership. Strong senior engineer, but the gap to a director-level AI strategy role is substantial.
